title: Going Live Checklist
intro: Launch day is here. You're about to share your task manager with the world. But first, let's make sure you haven't forgotten anything critical.

teach.title: The Launch Checklist
teach.body: Before going live, you need to verify: all environment variables are set, HTTPS is enabled, error tracking is configured, there's a way to roll back if something goes wrong, and someone is available to fix issues. Most launch-day disasters come from skipping one of these basics.

teach.title: Going Live Is Like Opening Night
teach.body: A theater company doesn't open the doors without checking the sound system, stage lights, and emergency exits. Going live is your opening night — the audience is real, first impressions matter, and a 10-minute walkthrough beforehand can save you from an embarrassing disaster.

teach.title: Pre-Launch Essentials
teach.code:
[ ] Production env vars set and verified
[ ] HTTPS enabled (automatic on Vercel)
[ ] Error tracking configured (Sentry, etc.)
[ ] Database migrations applied
[ ] Auth working in production environment
[ ] Rollback plan: know how to revert to previous deploy
[ ] Open Graph / social meta tags set
[ ] 404 page configured
teach.annotation: Walk through each item. A 10-minute checklist review can prevent a 10-hour launch-day emergency.

teach.body: Launch day is when your vibecoded project meets the real world. A smooth launch builds trust, attracts early adopters, and sets the stage for your first paying customers.
Launch small, launch ready. Don't announce to thousands if you haven't tested the production environment. Share with 10 people first, fix what breaks, then open the floodgates.

start.narrative: It's 9 AM on launch day. Your task manager is deployed to Vercel and everything looks good. You're about to share the link on social media. But you haven't tested the production environment yet — only development. What do you do?

choices.text: Post the link now — it worked in development, it should be fine
Do a quick smoke test: sign up, create a task, complete it, delete it — all in production
Delay the launch by a week to do extensive QA testing

yolo-launch.narrative: You post the link. Within 30 minutes, someone tries to sign up and gets a blank screen. The Supabase auth redirect URL is still set to localhost. You scramble to fix it while people are tweeting about your broken app. First impressions are lost.
yolo-launch.feedback: A 5-minute production smoke test would have caught the auth redirect issue before anyone else saw it. Always test the real environment before announcing your launch.

smoke-test.narrative: Good call. You try to sign up and the auth flow fails — the OAuth redirect URL is set to localhost. You fix it in the Supabase dashboard, retest, and everything works. What's next before launching?

choices.text: Share with 5 friends first, fix any issues they find, then do the public launch
Everything works now — launch publicly immediately

over-delay.narrative: A week-long delay for a side project kills your momentum. By the time you launch, you've lost interest. You compromise: do a 2-hour focused test session today and launch this afternoon.

choices.text: Run through the critical flows in production and launch today

soft-launch.narrative: Your friends find two small issues: a typo on the homepage and a confusing button label. You fix both in 20 minutes, redeploy, and launch publicly with confidence. The launch goes smoothly.
soft-launch.feedback: A soft launch with trusted testers is the gold standard. They find the embarrassing issues before the public does, and you can fix them calmly instead of scrambling.

quick-launch.narrative: The launch goes mostly well. A few users report minor UI issues, but nothing is broken. You fix issues as they come in.
quick-launch.feedback: An acceptable launch. The smoke test saved you from the auth bug, but a soft launch with friends would have caught the remaining UI issues too.
